task: I’m comparing a couple of used car options and one of them looks good, but the seller wants a quick commitment. Can you help me make a simple go/no-go checklist so I don’t rush into something expensive?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple **go / no-go used car checklist** you can use before committing fast.

## Go only if all of these are true
- **Clean title**: No salvage, rebuilt, flood, lemon, or branded title.
- **Vehicle history looks normal**: No major accidents, odometer issues, or repeated ownership red flags.
- **Price makes sense**: Compared with similar cars in your area, it’s not wildly overpriced.
- **Inspection is allowed**: Seller lets you get a **pre-purchase inspection** by an independent mechanic.
- **No warning lights**: Check engine, ABS, airbag, or other dash lights are off.
- **Starts cold and runs smoothly**: No rough idle, smoke, hesitation, or strange noises.
- **Transmission feels normal**: No slipping, hard shifting, jerking, or delay.
- **Brakes and steering feel solid**: No pulling, grinding, vibration, or clunks.
- **Tires and fluids look decent**: Even tire wear, no leaks, clean-ish oil/coolant, no burnt smells.
- **Maintenance records exist**: At least some proof of regular oil changes and service.
- **You have time to think**: Seller isn’t pressuring you to skip inspection or paperwork.

## No-go if any of these happen
- Seller refuses a mechanic inspection.
- Title is unclear, missing, or branded.
- Seller won’t share VIN or history report.
- Odometer, accident, or ownership story doesn’t add up.
- Major mechanical issue shows up on test drive.
- Price is “too good to be true” and the seller is rushing you.
- Payment is requested in a suspicious way only.
- You feel pressured to decide before verifying anything.

## 5-minute quick check before saying yes
1. Get the **VIN**.
2. Run a **history report**.
3. Compare price to similar listings.
4. Do a **test drive**.
5. Schedule a **mechanic inspection**.

## Simple rule
If you can’t verify the **title, condition, and price**, it’s a **no-go**.

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page printable checklist** or help you evaluate a specific car listing.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this works. Clean and usable — the title/history/inspection/pressure checks are the main ones I’d want anyway. The simple rule at the end is solid.

If I’m being picky, I’d probably add one line for **payment method** — no weird Zelle/cash-app-only stuff, no rushing a deposit before inspection. That’s the kind of thing that trips people up fast.

But overall, this is good enough to use right away.
